nodes.title: Release build, distribution, and LLM agent integration
nodes.description: Set up cross-platform release builds (GoReleaser), CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions), distribution channels (Homebrew tap), Claude Code plugin for /plugin discovery, and LLM agent kit with MCP configs and skill files. Includes supply chain security (cosign signing, reproducible builds, pinned dependencies) and release documentation (VDD, build environment baseline, metrics).
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: GoReleaser and CI/CD pipeline
nodes.description: Set up automated cross-platform builds and continuous integration. GoReleaser produces 6 platform binaries (darwin/linux/windows x amd64/arm64) with cosign signing. GitHub Actions runs tests, lint, and coverage on every push, and publishes releases on tag push.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Create .goreleaser.yaml with cosign signing
nodes.description: GoReleaser config for cross-platform binary releases with Sigstore cosign signing for supply chain integrity.
nodes.prompt: Create .goreleaser.yaml at repo root. Build ./cmd/mtix/ with CGO_ENABLED=0 and -trimpath. Targets: darwin/linux/windows x amd64/arm64. Use CommitDate (not Date) for reproducible builds in ldflags: -X main.version={{.Version}} -X main.commit={{.ShortCommit}} -X main.date={{.CommitDate}}. Archives: tar.gz for darwin/linux, zip for windows, include LICENSE, NOTICE, README.md. Checksums: sha256 in checksums.txt. Add signs: section with cosign sign-blob (keyless OIDC). Add extra_files for mtix-agent-kit tarball. Verify with: goreleaser release --snapshot --clean.
nodes.acceptance: 1. goreleaser release --snapshot --clean produces 6 binaries. 2. Each binary shows correct version via ./mtix version. 3. Checksums.txt is generated. 4. Two consecutive builds produce identical binary checksums (reproducible). 5. cosign signs section is present in config.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Create CI workflow
nodes.description: GitHub Actions CI pipeline running tests, lint, and build on every push and PR.
nodes.prompt: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml. Triggers: push to main, pull_request to main. All actions pinned to commit SHAs (not tags). Add permissions: contents: read at workflow level. Jobs: (1) test-go: matrix ubuntu-22.04 + macos-latest, setup-go 1.25, go mod verify, go test ./... -race -count=1. (2) test-web: setup-node 22, cd web && npm ci && npx vitest run. (3) lint: golangci-lint-action pinned to SHA. (4) build: depends on test-go, test-web, lint; builds web (npm ci && npm run build), copies dist to internal/web/dist/, runs go build -trimpath ./cmd/mtix/, verifies ./mtix version output. Lock all runners to ubuntu-22.04 (not ubuntu-latest).
nodes.acceptance: 1. CI runs on push to main. 2. CI runs on PRs. 3. All actions pinned to SHA with tag comment. 4. permissions: contents: read is set. 5. Tests, lint, and build all execute. 6. Runner images are pinned versions.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Create release workflow
nodes.description: GitHub Actions release pipeline triggered by version tags, with quality gates and manual approval.
nodes.prompt: Create .github/workflows/release.yml. Trigger: push tag v*.*.*. Permissions: contents: write, id-token: write (for cosign OIDC). Jobs: (1) preflight: go test -race, go test -coverprofile, npm ci && npx vitest run, golangci-lint, go mod verify. Tag-based coverage gate: if tag contains -beta or -rc, min 85%; else min 90%. Fail if git working tree is dirty. (2) release: environment: production (manual approval gate). Build web UI. SHA256 the web dist/ directory. npm audit --audit-level=high as hard gate. Generate BUILD-ENVIRONMENT.json (Go version, Node version, GoReleaser version, runner OS, commit hash). Run scripts/build-agent-kit.sh. Independent reference build: go build with same ldflags, compare checksum to GoReleaser output (BEFORE signing). Run goreleaser release --clean. Export mtix tasks.json and test results as release artifacts. Run scripts/generate-vdd.sh. (3) update-homebrew: repository-dispatch to hyper-swe/homebrew-tap. All actions pinned to commit SHAs.
nodes.acceptance: 1. Workflow triggers on v*.*.* tags only. 2. Manual approval gate via environment: production. 3. Coverage gate is tag-aware (85% beta, 90% GA). 4. Dirty tree check fails the build. 5. Web dist SHA256 is computed. 6. npm audit is a hard gate. 7. Dual-build comparison runs before signing. 8. BUILD-ENVIRONMENT.json is generated. 9. VDD is generated. 10. Tasks.json exported as artifact.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Add Dependabot config for GitHub Actions
nodes.description: Auto-update pinned action SHAs when new versions are released.
nodes.prompt: Create .github/dependabot.yml with package-ecosystem: github-actions, directory: /, schedule: interval: weekly.
nodes.acceptance: 1. File exists at .github/dependabot.yml. 2. Configured for github-actions ecosystem. 3. Weekly update schedule.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Update .gitignore and Makefile for release targets
nodes.description: Add dist/ to .gitignore and add release-related Makefile targets.
nodes.prompt: Add dist/ to .gitignore. Add Makefile targets: generate-plugin-skills (renders templates to .claude-plugin/skills/), agent-kit (builds the agent kit tarball). Ensure npm ci is used instead of npm install in all Makefile web targets.
nodes.acceptance: 1. dist/ is in .gitignore. 2. make generate-plugin-skills works. 3. make agent-kit produces tarball. 4. All web targets use npm ci.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Fix Windows cross-compilation for syscall.Flock
nodes.description: GoReleaser Windows builds fail because sync_service.go uses Unix-only syscall.Flock. Extract file locking into build-tagged platform files.
nodes.prompt: Move fileFd, acquireLock, releaseLock from internal/service/sync_service.go into flock_unix.go (//go:build !windows) and flock_windows.go (//go:build windows). Add lockShared and lockExclusive constants to replace direct syscall.LOCK_SH/LOCK_EX usage. Remove syscall import from sync_service.go. Update callers to use lockShared/lockExclusive constants.
nodes.acceptance: 1. goreleaser release --snapshot --clean builds all 6 targets including Windows. 2. Unix tests pass. 3. No syscall import in sync_service.go.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Release documentation and compliance artifacts
nodes.description: Auto-generate release documentation: Version Description Document (VDD) with changelog, artifact inventory, known issues, and upgrade instructions. Build environment baseline (BUILD-ENVIRONMENT.json) recording exact tool versions. Metrics report with per-package coverage.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Create VDD generation script
nodes.description: Auto-generate a Version Description Document for each release containing version, changelog, artifact inventory, known issues, metrics, and upgrade/rollback instructions.
nodes.prompt: Create scripts/generate-vdd.sh. Inputs: git tag, checksums.txt path, mtix binary path. Outputs: VDD-v{version}.md. Sections: (1) Version identifier and date. (2) Changelog from git log between previous tag and current tag. (3) Artifact inventory with checksums from checksums.txt. (4) Known issues from mtix list --status open,blocked --json. (5) Per-package coverage from go test -coverprofile. (6) Test count and pass rate. (7) Upgrade instructions: mtix export, install new binary, mtix verify. (8) Rollback: reinstall old binary, mtix import backup.
nodes.acceptance: 1. Script produces valid markdown. 2. Changelog section populated from git log. 3. Known issues populated from mtix. 4. Metrics section has coverage by package. 5. Upgrade/rollback instructions present.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Create BUILD-ENVIRONMENT.json generation
nodes.description: Record exact build tool versions as a release artifact for reproducibility.
nodes.prompt: In the release workflow, generate BUILD-ENVIRONMENT.json containing: go_version (from go version), node_version (from node --version), goreleaser_version, runner_os, runner_arch, commit_hash, tag_name, build_timestamp (SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH from git commit). Write as a step in release.yml, attach as release artifact.
nodes.acceptance: 1. JSON file contains all specified fields. 2. Attached as release artifact. 3. go_version matches the setup-go version.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Add release metrics and traceability exports
nodes.description: Export mtix task state and test results as release artifacts for requirements traceability.
nodes.prompt: In the release workflow, add steps: (1) mtix export to produce tasks.json, attach as release artifact. (2) go test -json ./... > test-results.json, attach as release artifact. (3) go test -coverprofile=coverage.out, attach as release artifact.
nodes.acceptance: 1. tasks.json attached to release. 2. test-results.json attached. 3. coverage.out attached.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Claude Code plugin
nodes.description: Create .claude-plugin/ directory structure with plugin manifest, marketplace metadata, MCP server config, and pre-rendered skill files so users can install mtix via /plugin marketplace add hyper-swe/mtix.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Create .claude-plugin/ structure and manifests
nodes.description: Plugin manifest and marketplace metadata for Claude Code /plugin discovery.
nodes.prompt: Create .claude-plugin/plugin.json: name mtix, version 0.1.0, description 'AI-native micro issue manager for code-generating LLMs', author HyperSWE, license Apache-2.0. Create .claude-plugin/marketplace.json: name mtix, categories [productivity, project-management], repository hyper-swe/mtix. Create .claude-plugin/.mcp.json: mcpServers.mtix with command mtix, args [mcp, --project, .]. All JSON must be valid and parseable.
nodes.acceptance: 1. plugin.json is valid JSON with all required fields. 2. marketplace.json is valid. 3. .mcp.json references mtix command. 4. jq validates all three files.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Generate static skill files for plugin
nodes.description: Pre-rendered skill Markdown files for the Claude Code plugin, using generic PROJ prefix.
nodes.prompt: Generate 5 skill files into .claude-plugin/skills/: mtix-task-execution.md, mtix-planning.md, mtix-review.md, mtix-multi-agent.md, mtix-admin.md. Generate 4 reference checklists into .claude-plugin/skills/references/: do-178c-checklist.md, iec-62304-checklist.md, nasa-std-8739-checklist.md, mil-std-498-checklist.md. Use the existing templates from internal/docs/templates/skills/ rendered with ProjectPrefix=PROJ as the generic placeholder. These are static committed files, not templates.
nodes.acceptance: 1. All 9 files exist in .claude-plugin/skills/. 2. Files contain rendered content (not Go template syntax). 3. ProjectPrefix shows as PROJ. 4. Files are valid Markdown.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Add generate-plugin-skills Makefile target
nodes.description: Makefile target to re-render plugin skill files from templates.
nodes.prompt: Add generate-plugin-skills target to Makefile. It should: build the mtix binary, then use it (or a Go helper) to render the skill templates with ProjectPrefix=PROJ to .claude-plugin/skills/. This allows refreshing static skills when templates change.
nodes.acceptance: 1. make generate-plugin-skills runs without error. 2. Output files match the manually generated ones. 3. Target is documented in Makefile help.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Add Codex plugin structure
nodes.description: Create .codex-plugin/ directory with plugin manifest, skills, and MCP config for OpenAI Codex plugin discovery.
nodes.prompt: Create .codex-plugin/plugin.json with name mtix, version 0.1.0, author HyperSWE, skills ./skills/, mcpServers ./.mcp.json. Create skills directory with SKILL.md files mirroring the Claude Code skills. Create .mcp.json referencing mtix MCP server. Create .agents/plugins/marketplace.json for repo-level Codex plugin discovery.
nodes.acceptance: 1. .codex-plugin/plugin.json is valid JSON. 2. Skills directory has SKILL.md files. 3. .mcp.json references mtix command. 4. .agents/plugins/marketplace.json lists mtix plugin.

nodes.title: LLM agent kit
nodes.description: Package downloadable agent kit (tar.gz) attached to each GitHub Release containing skill files, MCP configs for multiple IDE clients, and Codex AGENTS.md. Includes per-file integrity manifest (MANIFEST.sha256).
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Create Codex AGENTS.md
nodes.description: Setup instructions for OpenAI Codex agents to use mtix for task management.
nodes.prompt: Create docs/codex/AGENTS.md. Sections: (1) Setup: install mtix, run mtix init --prefix PROJ. (2) Task workflow: mtix list --status ready, mtix context <id>, mtix claim <id> --agent codex, do work, mtix done <id>. (3) Rules: always read context chain before work, never skip task creation, report blockers with mtix blocked. Keep instructions concise and actionable for an LLM agent.
nodes.acceptance: 1. File exists at docs/codex/AGENTS.md. 2. Contains setup, workflow, and rules sections. 3. All mtix commands are correct and functional.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Create agent kit build script with MANIFEST.sha256
nodes.description: Script to package the LLM agent kit tarball with per-file integrity manifest.
nodes.prompt: Create scripts/build-agent-kit.sh. Takes VERSION as argument. Creates mtix-agent-kit-v{VERSION}/ directory containing: skills/ (copied from .claude-plugin/skills/), mcp-config/ (copied from docs/mcp-config/), codex/AGENTS.md (copied from docs/codex/), README.md (setup instructions). Generates MANIFEST.sha256 with sha256sum of every file. Creates mtix-agent-kit-v{VERSION}.tar.gz in dist/.
nodes.acceptance: 1. Script runs with VERSION=0.1.0. 2. Tarball contains all expected directories. 3. MANIFEST.sha256 lists every file with correct hashes. 4. Tarball is in dist/.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Add agent-kit Makefile target
nodes.description: Makefile target to build the LLM agent kit tarball.
nodes.prompt: Add agent-kit target to Makefile that calls scripts/build-agent-kit.sh with the current VERSION. Depends on generate-plugin-skills (skills must be rendered first).
nodes.acceptance: 1. make agent-kit produces tarball in dist/. 2. Depends on generate-plugin-skills. 3. VERSION is injected from git tag.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Homebrew tap
nodes.description: Create hyper-swe/homebrew-tap repo with multi-platform formula and auto-update workflow triggered by repository-dispatch from the release pipeline.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Create hyper-swe/homebrew-tap repo and formula
nodes.description: Homebrew tap repository with multi-platform formula for brew install hyper-swe/tap/mtix.
nodes.prompt: Create Formula/mtix.rb in hyper-swe/homebrew-tap repo. Formula uses on_macos/on_linux blocks with on_arm/on_intel sub-blocks. URLs point to GitHub Releases tar.gz archives. SHA256 placeholders to be filled by automation. Includes test block: assert_match version.to_s, shell_output(bin/mtix version). License Apache-2.0. Description: AI-native micro issue manager for code-generating LLMs.
nodes.acceptance: 1. Formula is valid Ruby. 2. Covers darwin (arm64/amd64) and linux (arm64/amd64). 3. Test block verifies version output. 4. brew audit --strict passes (after SHA256 values are real).
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Add tap update workflow
nodes.description: GitHub Actions workflow in the tap repo that auto-updates the formula on new mtix releases.
nodes.prompt: Create .github/workflows/update-formula.yml in hyper-swe/homebrew-tap. Trigger: repository_dispatch type: release-published. Steps: download checksums.txt from the release URL in the dispatch payload, parse per-platform SHA256 values, update Formula/mtix.rb with sed or a script, commit and push. Use a fine-grained PAT scoped only to the tap repo.
nodes.acceptance: 1. Workflow triggers on repository_dispatch. 2. Formula SHA256 values are updated. 3. Commit is pushed to main.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: README and documentation updates
nodes.description: Add Install section to README.md covering Homebrew, binary download, go install, and Claude Code plugin. Update QUALITY-STANDARDS.md with beta/GA coverage gate thresholds.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Add Install section to README.md
nodes.description: Add installation instructions to README covering all distribution channels.
nodes.prompt: Add an Install section to README.md after the project description, before Features. Include: Homebrew (brew install hyper-swe/tap/mtix), Binary Download (link to GitHub Releases), Go Install (go install github.com/hyper-swe/mtix/cmd/mtix@latest), Claude Code Plugin (/plugin marketplace add hyper-swe/mtix).
nodes.acceptance: 1. Install section exists in README. 2. All 4 installation methods listed. 3. Commands are correct and copy-pasteable.

nodes.title: Update QUALITY-STANDARDS.md with beta/GA coverage gates
nodes.description: Document the tiered coverage thresholds: 85% for beta releases, 90% for GA.
nodes.prompt: Update QUALITY-STANDARDS.md coverage section to document: beta releases (tags with -beta or -rc suffix) require 85% minimum coverage, GA releases (clean semver tags) require 90% minimum. Add rationale: beta threshold allows shipping while coverage is raised between beta and GA.
nodes.acceptance: 1. Coverage section documents both thresholds. 2. Tag-based enforcement is explained. 3. Rationale is included.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Security hardening
nodes.description: Harden input validation and output sanitization: SafeMarkdown HTML tag filtering, plugin template prefix validation, import safety checks, HTTP origin validation.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Sanitize project prefix in plugin template renderer
nodes.description: Validate project prefix format before template rendering for defense-in-depth.
nodes.prompt: In internal/docs/plugin.go, in the BuildTemplateData or render function, add validation that ProjectPrefix matches ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{1,9}$ before passing to templates. Return an error if invalid. The prefix is already validated at creation time, but this adds a second check at render time.
nodes.acceptance: 1. Invalid prefix (e.g., containing newlines or special chars) is rejected at render time. 2. Valid prefix PROJ passes. 3. Test added for invalid prefix rejection.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Add go mod verify and npm ci enforcement
nodes.description: Ensure dependency integrity verification in build pipeline.
nodes.prompt: This is implemented within the CI workflows (MTIX-1.1.2 and MTIX-1.1.3): add go mod verify step before tests, set GOFLAGS=-mod=readonly, use npm ci instead of npm install in all CI steps and Makefile web targets. Mark this task done when those workflows include these steps.
nodes.acceptance: 1. go mod verify runs in CI before tests. 2. GOFLAGS=-mod=readonly is set. 3. All npm commands use ci not install.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Add SVG to SafeMarkdown HTML tag strip list
nodes.description: Prevent SVG-based cross-site scripting in the web UI markdown renderer.
nodes.prompt: In web/src/components/SafeMarkdown.tsx, find the sanitizeHTML function's tag strip list (which strips script, iframe, style). Add svg to the list. The regex pattern should match <svg, </svg>, and <svg with attributes (e.g., <svg onload=...>).
nodes.acceptance: 1. <svg onload=alert(1)> is stripped from rendered output. 2. Normal markdown rendering still works. 3. Existing SafeMarkdown tests pass. 4. New test added for SVG stripping.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Reject zero-node import when database is non-empty
nodes.description: Prevent accidental data wipe from importing an empty tasks.json.
nodes.prompt: In the import function (internal/store/sqlite/import.go or internal/service/sync_service.go), add a check: if the import data has 0 nodes and the database has >0 nodes, return an error unless a force flag is set. Add --force flag to the mtix import CLI command (cmd/mtix/sync.go) that passes through to the import function.
nodes.acceptance: 1. Import with 0 nodes and non-empty DB returns error. 2. Import with --force flag succeeds. 3. Import with 0 nodes and empty DB succeeds (no false positive). 4. Auto-import (git pull trigger) also rejects empty imports. 5. Tests added for all cases.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Replace CORS wildcard with localhost origin validation
nodes.description: Restrict cross-origin API access to localhost origins only.
nodes.prompt: In internal/api/http/middleware.go, CORSMiddleware function, replace Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * with origin-based validation. Read the Origin header from the request. If origin is empty (same-origin or non-browser client) or starts with http://localhost or http://127.0.0.1, set Access-Control-Allow-Origin to that origin. Otherwise, do not set the header (browser will reject the cross-origin request). Existing tests check for Allow-Methods and Allow-Headers but NOT for Allow-Origin: *, so they will pass without modification.
nodes.acceptance: 1. Same-origin requests work (no Origin header). 2. localhost origins are allowed. 3. External origins (e.g., https://evil.com) are rejected. 4. Existing CORS tests pass. 5. New test added for origin rejection.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Add WebSocket origin validation against localhost
nodes.description: Prevent cross-origin WebSocket connections from external websites.
nodes.prompt: In internal/api/http/websocket.go, update the wsUpgrader CheckOrigin function. If Origin header is empty (Go clients, non-browser), return true. If Origin starts with http://localhost or http://127.0.0.1, return true. Otherwise return false. Go websocket test clients (websocket.DefaultDialer) do not send Origin headers, so all existing tests pass.
nodes.acceptance: 1. Browser connections from localhost origin succeed. 2. Non-browser clients (no Origin header) succeed. 3. Browser connections from external origins are rejected (HTTP 403 on upgrade). 4. All existing WebSocket tests pass. 5. New test added for origin rejection.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Developer environment setup
nodes.description: Add tool version pinning file for consistent development environments across contributors.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.description: Pin development tool versions for consistent environments across contributors.

nodes.acceptance: 1. File exists at .tool-versions. 2. Contains golang, nodejs, golangci-lint with exact versions. 3. Versions match what the project actually uses.

nodes.title: Publish mtix to Docker MCP Catalog
nodes.description: List mtix as a verified MCP server in the Docker MCP Catalog (hub.docker.com/mcp/explore). This gives mtix one-click install in Docker Desktop MCP Toolkit, working with Claude Desktop, VS Code, Cursor, and Windsurf without manual config. Docker signs, builds, and distributes the image as mcp/mtix with container isolation, provenance tracking, and SBOMs.
nodes.prompt:
SCOPE OF WORK

Prerequisites (all met):
- Apache-2.0 license (required; GPL rejected by Docker)
- MCP compliant via mtix mcp (stdio transport)
- Active development, documented, security contact via GitHub issues

DELIVERABLES

1. Dockerfile (in mtix repo root)
   - Multi-stage build: golang:1.25-alpine builder + alpine:3.21 runtime
   - CGO_ENABLED=0, -trimpath for reproducibility
   - Web UI must be built and embedded (cd web && npm ci && npm run build)
   - ENTRYPOINT ["mtix", "mcp", "--project", "/workspace"]
   - /workspace is the mount point for user project directory

2. server.yaml (for docker/mcp-registry PR)
   Location: servers/mtix/server.yaml in forked docker/mcp-registry

   name: mtix
   image: mcp/mtix
   type: server
   meta:
     category: devops
     tags: [issue-tracking, task-management, ai-agents, llm]
   about:
     title: mtix
     description: AI-native micro issue manager for code-generating LLMs
     icon: https://github.com/hyper-swe.png
   source:
     project: https://github.com/hyper-swe/mtix
     commit: <HEAD commit at submission time>
   run:
     volumes:
       - "{{mtix.project_dir}}:/workspace"
     disableNetwork: true
   config:
     description: Path to the project directory containing .mtix/
     parameters:
       type: object
       properties:
         project_dir:
           type: string
           title: Project Directory
           description: Path to the project containing .mtix/ directory
       required:
         - project_dir

3. tools.json (optional, for catalog display)
   List of mtix MCP tools with names and descriptions, extracted from
   the tool registry. Only needed if Docker build cannot list tools
   without a live project directory.

4. readme.md (for catalog entry)
   Short description for the Docker Hub mcp/mtix page. Can be derived
   from README.md.

VOLUME MOUNT DESIGN

Docker MCP Toolkit pattern: user-configurable single directory mount.
- User provides project_dir in Docker Desktop UI
- Container mounts it at /workspace
- mtix mcp --project /workspace accesses .mtix/data/mtix.db and
  .mtix/tasks.json inside the mounted directory
- disableNetwork: true (mtix MCP needs no network access)
- No secrets needed (local-only tool)

This matches the filesystem and git server patterns in the catalog.

SUBMISSION PROCESS

1. Fork docker/mcp-registry
2. Create servers/mtix/ directory with server.yaml, tools.json, readme.md
3. Run: task validate -- --name mtix
4. Run: task build -- --tools mtix
5. Test locally: docker mcp catalog import catalogs/mtix/catalog.yaml
6. Open PR to docker/mcp-registry
7. Docker team reviews, builds, signs, publishes as mcp/mtix
8. Available in Docker Desktop MCP Toolkit within 24 hours of merge

TESTING

- Build Dockerfile locally: docker build -t mtix-mcp .
- Run with volume: docker run -v /path/to/project:/workspace mtix-mcp
- Verify tools list: echo "{}" | docker run -i -v /path/to/project:/workspace mtix-mcp
- Verify mtix_create, mtix_list, mtix_briefing, mtix_context tools work
- Verify .mtix/data/mtix.db is created/read inside mounted directory
- Verify tasks.json sync works through the mount

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

1. Dockerfile builds successfully with make build-web && go build
2. docker run -v PROJECT:/workspace mtix-mcp lists all 37 MCP tools
3. task validate passes in docker/mcp-registry fork
4. task build passes in docker/mcp-registry fork
5. PR submitted to docker/mcp-registry
6. Local testing confirms create/list/briefing/context tools work through Docker
7. .mtix/data/mtix.db persists across container restarts via volume mount
8. No network access required (disableNetwork: true verified)
nodes.acceptance: 1. Dockerfile builds and produces working mtix MCP server. 2. server.yaml validates in docker/mcp-registry. 3. PR submitted to docker/mcp-registry. 4. Local testing confirms all MCP tools work through Docker volume mount. 5. Database persistence verified across container restarts.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: sync --fix doesn't refresh sentinel hashes when content matches
nodes.description: When DB and tasks.json have the same node IDs but the sentinel hash files (.mtix/data/sync.sha256, sync-db.sha256) are stale, mtix sync --fix reports 'In sync' and exits without refreshing the sentinels. The conflict-detection WARN in mtix list persists indefinitely. Reported by external user.
nodes.prompt:
ROOT CAUSE

cmd/mtix/sync.go:58 has:
    if fix && !report.InSync {
        if exportErr := app.syncSvc.AutoExport(ctx, app.mtixDir); exportErr != nil {

The AutoExport (which refreshes sync.sha256, sync-db.sha256, and meta last_export_hash) is only called when InSync=false. But Compare() (sync_service.go:410-479) only checks node ID set equality — it does not read or compare the sentinel files.

So when:
- DB and tasks.json have identical node IDs → InSync=true
- Sentinel hash files are stale (different hash from current content)
- AutoImport conflict detector keeps warning forever

The user runs sync --fix expecting the warning to clear; nothing happens.

FIX

Drop the !report.InSync guard. When --fix is passed, always call AutoExport. AutoExport is deterministic per FR-15.3a (sorted nodes, fixed JSON key order, UTC timestamps) so calling it on equivalent content produces byte-identical output (modulo exported_at). It refreshes both sentinels and the meta last_export_hash. Idempotent.

cmd/mtix/sync.go:58 should change from:
    if fix && !report.InSync {
to:
    if fix {

The user-visible message should reflect both cases:
    if fix {
        if exportErr := ... {
            return ...
        }
        if report.InSync {
            fmt.Println("Sentinels refreshed: tasks.json re-exported.")
        } else {
            fmt.Println("Sync fixed: tasks.json updated from database.")
        }
    }

TESTS (TDD red first)

1. TestRunSync_FixWithStaleSentinels_RefreshesAndClearsWarning
   - Create DB with nodes
   - AutoExport (writes valid sentinels + tasks.json)
   - Manually corrupt sync.sha256 to "stale_hash"
   - Run sync --fix → assert sentinels are refreshed to current values
   - Verify subsequent AutoImport does not warn about conflict

2. TestRunSync_FixWithoutDrift_StillRefreshes
   - Setup synced state
   - Run sync --fix → AutoExport called → exit 0
   - Verify sentinels written

3. TestRunSync_FixWithContentDrift_RewritesTasksJSON
   - DB has node A, tasks.json has node B
   - Run sync --fix → tasks.json now matches DB

ACCEPTANCE
1. All 3 new tests pass.
2. Existing sync tests still pass.
3. Coverage on cmd/mtix/sync.go runSync >= 90%.
4. Manual repro of user scenario confirms warning clears after sync --fix.
nodes.acceptance: 1. Tests pass. 2. Existing tests still pass. 3. mtix sync --fix refreshes sentinels even when content matches. 4. Conflict detection warning clears after sync --fix in user scenario.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Export node_type asymmetric with import — round-trip not idempotent
nodes.description: MTIX-7 (v0.1.1-beta) made import derive node_type from depth for tamper resistance. But export was untouched and still writes the raw DB column verbatim. So a DB with legacy node_type values (from pre-v0.1.1-beta) exports legacy values; on next import they get normalized — appearing as random 'flips' (e.g., 44 of 140 nodes for a real user). Round-trip is not idempotent. Reported by external user.
nodes.prompt:
ROOT CAUSE

MTIX-7 (commit 7ad4040) added depth-derivation to IMPORT but left EXPORT unchanged.

Import (internal/store/sqlite/import.go:232,255):
    n.NodeType = string(model.NodeTypeForDepth(n.Depth))

Export (internal/store/sqlite/export.go:153):
    COALESCE(node_type, "auto")  -- reads raw DB column

Asymmetry: import normalizes, export persists. So a DB with legacy node_type values exports legacy values; subsequent import re-normalizes them. Round-trip is not idempotent.

User reported: 44 of 140 nodes had node_type flipped after import --mode replace, with the pattern matching depth-derived values (depth 0 → epic, depth 1 → story).

FIX

Make export also derive node_type from depth, matching import. Both ends produce canonical depth-derived values.

internal/store/sqlite/export.go exportNodes(): after scanning rows, override n.NodeType with model.NodeTypeForDepth(n.Depth). Or change the SELECT to not bother reading node_type from DB (just compute from depth in Go).

Cleaner approach — in the loop over rows.Next() after scanning:
    n.NodeType = string(model.NodeTypeForDepth(n.Depth))

This:
- Maintains tamper resistance (export cannot write user-controllable types either)
- Makes round-trip truly idempotent
- Self-heals legacy DBs on next export (one-time migration)
- Matches the symmetry the user is asking for in the bug report

TESTS (TDD red first)

1. TestExportNodes_NodeTypeDerivedFromDepth
   - Insert nodes with intentionally wrong node_type in DB (e.g., depth=0 with stored type=story)
   - Call Export
   - Assert exported node_type = epic (depth-derived), not story (DB value)

2. TestExportImport_RoundTrip_NodeTypeIdempotent
   - Insert nodes
   - Export → write to file
   - Import (replace mode)
   - Export again → assert second export matches first export byte-for-byte (modulo exported_at timestamp)

3. TestExport_LegacyNodeType_NormalizedOnExport
   - Simulate pre-v0.1.1-beta DB state (depth 0 stored as story)
   - Export
   - Verify exported node_type is epic (new convention, depth-derived)

ACCEPTANCE
1. All 3 new tests pass.
2. All existing export/import tests still pass.
3. Coverage on internal/store/sqlite/export.go exportNodes >= 90%.
4. Round-trip from same DB twice produces byte-identical output (modulo timestamp).
5. Documentation: add note to FR-7.8 / FR-15.3a noting that node_type is derived from depth at both export and import.
nodes.acceptance: 1. Tests pass. 2. Existing tests still pass. 3. Round-trip is byte-identical (modulo exported_at). 4. Legacy node_type values normalized on first export. 5. Documentation updated.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Fix SQLite concurrency: busy_timeout on reads + BEGIN IMMEDIATE
nodes.description: Add busy_timeout to read connection and use BEGIN IMMEDIATE for write transactions to prevent database-is-locked errors during concurrent access.
nodes.prompt: In internal/store/sqlite/store.go openDB function: (A) Add PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000 for read connections (currently only on write). (B) For write connections, change sql.Open DSN from bare path to path?_txlock=immediate so all write transactions use BEGIN IMMEDIATE. This ensures busy_timeout is always applied (BEGIN DEFERRED bypasses it on lock upgrade). No changes to WithTx in tx.go needed.
nodes.acceptance: 1. Read connection has busy_timeout set. 2. Write connection DSN includes _txlock=immediate. 3. All tests pass. 4. Concurrent mtix show + mtix create does not produce database-is-locked errors.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Wrap SQLite lock timeout errors with actionable guidance
nodes.description: When busy_timeout is exhausted, wrap the error with retry guidance for agents.
nodes.prompt: In internal/store/sqlite/tx.go WithTx function, check if the BeginTx error contains 'database is locked' or SQLITE_BUSY. If so, wrap with: 'another mtix operation is in progress, retry in a moment'. Also check the Commit error path.

nodes.title: Add --changed-since filter to list and query commands
nodes.description: Enables sync agents to poll efficiently: 'what changed since my last check?' Without this, agents must fetch all nodes and diff against their own state. Index on updated_at already exists (idx_nodes_updated).
nodes.prompt: Add --changed-since flag to mtix list and mtix query commands. Accept ISO 8601 timestamp. Implementation: add ChangedSince time.Time field to store.NodeFilter. In internal/store/sqlite/node_list.go buildFilterClauses, add WHERE clause: updated_at > ? with parameterized timestamp value. Also support relative durations (5m, 1h, 24h) parsed in the CLI layer. Add --json support. Files: cmd/mtix/query.go (flag), internal/store/store.go (NodeFilter), internal/store/sqlite/node_list.go (WHERE clause).
nodes.acceptance: 1. mtix list --changed-since 2026-04-06T10:00:00Z returns only nodes modified after that time. 2. mtix list --changed-since 1h returns nodes modified in the last hour. 3. Parameterized query (no SQL injection). 4. Works with --json flag. 5. Works with other filters (--status, --assignee) simultaneously. 6. Test coverage for the new filter.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Add outgoing webhook support for state change notifications
nodes.description: Generic webhook system for push-based notifications. When a node changes state, mtix fires an HTTP POST to configured URLs. Enables event-driven sync with external tools without mtix knowing anything about Jira/Linear/GitHub Issues.
nodes.prompt:
Implementation requirements from security and performance audit:

Config (.mtix/config.yaml):
  webhooks:
    - url: https://example.com/webhook
      secret: hmac-secret
      events: [node.created, node.done, node.cancelled, node.blocked]
      payload: minimal  # or full

Core requirements:
1. ASYNC delivery — dispatch from event broadcaster goroutine, NEVER inside a DB transaction (WH-01)
2. Minimal default payload: {event, node_id, status, project, timestamp}. Full payload (includes title, assignee) only when payload: full is set (WH-02)
3. HMAC-SHA256 signature in X-Mtix-Signature header using per-webhook secret (WH-03)
4. 100ms debounce window — collect events, send as batch array to prevent bulk operation floods (WH-04)
5. Config-file only — not API-configurable (WH-05)
6. Warn at startup if webhook URL is non-HTTPS and non-localhost (WH-06)
7. 5-second timeout per delivery
8. 3 retries with exponential backoff (1s, 5s, 25s), then drop with warning log
9. HTTP client with connection pooling (reuse connections to same URL)

Files: internal/service/config.go (webhook config struct), internal/service/broadcaster.go (webhook dispatch), internal/service/webhook.go (new — HTTP client, HMAC, batching), cmd/mtix/root.go (config loading and validation).
nodes.acceptance: 1. Webhook fires on configured events. 2. Payload matches configured mode (minimal/full). 3. X-Mtix-Signature header present and verifiable. 4. Slow endpoint does not block mtix operations. 5. Bulk decompose (50 children) produces batched webhook, not 50 individual calls. 6. Non-HTTPS non-localhost URL produces startup warning. 7. Failed delivery retries 3 times then logs warning. 8. Config-file only — no API endpoint to add webhooks. 9. Test coverage for delivery, HMAC, batching, timeout, retry.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Align node type hierarchy with industry convention
nodes.description: mtix has Epic below Story which is inverted from Agile/Scrum/Jira/Linear convention. Swap depth-to-type mapping: depth 0 = epic (was story), depth 1 = story (was epic). Issue and micro unchanged. Derive node_type from depth on import for tamper resistance and cross-version compatibility.
nodes.prompt: Three code changes: (1) In internal/model/types.go NodeTypeForDepth, swap case 0 to return NodeTypeEpic and case 1 to return NodeTypeStory. Update constant comments. (2) In internal/store/sqlite/import.go insertExportNode and mergeImportNode, set node_type to NodeTypeForDepth(n.Depth) instead of trusting the file value. (3) Fix 26 test references: 21 NodeTypeStory at depth 0 → NodeTypeEpic, 5 NodeTypeEpic at depth 1 → NodeTypeStory. 132 Issue/Micro refs unchanged. Update README, USERMANUAL, CONTRIBUTING. Regenerate docs.
nodes.acceptance: 1. NodeTypeForDepth(0) returns epic. 2. NodeTypeForDepth(1) returns story. 3. Import derives type from depth, ignores file value. 4. All tests pass. 5. Coverage >= 87%. 6. golangci-lint clean. 7. Build succeeds. 8. Documentation updated.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Skip redundant auto-import via meta table export hash
nodes.description: Auto-import triggers on every command when tasks.json hash differs from stored hash. With multiple agents on the same machine, each mutation triggers an export (hash changes) which causes all other agents to do a full ImportModeReplace — a thundering herd problem. Fix: store last export hash in the meta table. If file hash matches last export hash, the file came from our own export — skip import. External changes (git pull) produce a different hash and import correctly.
nodes.prompt:
TDD approach — write failing test first.

TEST (in internal/service/sync_service_test.go):
TestAutoImport_SkipsWhenFileMatchesOwnExport: Create a sync service, insert nodes, call AutoExport (writes tasks.json + sets meta last_export_hash), then call AutoImport. Import should be skipped because the file hash matches the export hash. Verify by checking that nodes were NOT reimported (no write transaction occurred).

TestAutoImport_ImportsWhenFileChangedExternally: Create a sync service, insert nodes, call AutoExport, then modify tasks.json externally (simulate git pull), then call AutoImport. Import SHOULD trigger because file hash differs from export hash. Verify by checking the imported data matches the modified file.

TestAutoImport_ImportsWhenNoExportHashExists: Fresh database with no last_export_hash in meta. Place a tasks.json file. AutoImport should trigger (fail-safe to current behavior).

TestAutoImport_SkipsWhenSiblingAgentExported: Agent A exports (sets meta hash). Agent B has not exported. Agent B runs AutoImport. Should skip because meta hash matches file hash (Agent A and B share the same DB).

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. In AutoExport (sync_service.go), after writing tasks.json and hash files, write the file hash to meta: store.WriteDB().ExecContext(ctx, INSERT OR REPLACE INTO meta (key, value) VALUES ('last_export_hash', ?), fileHash)

2. In AutoImport (sync_service.go), after detecting file hash differs from stored hash, check meta: SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key = 'last_export_hash'. If file hash matches meta value, log at DEBUG level and return nil (skip import).

3. Fail-safe: if meta read fails or returns no row, proceed with import (current behavior).

Files: internal/service/sync_service.go, internal/service/sync_service_test.go
nodes.acceptance: 1. TestAutoImport_SkipsWhenFileMatchesOwnExport passes. 2. TestAutoImport_ImportsWhenFileChangedExternally passes. 3. TestAutoImport_ImportsWhenNoExportHashExists passes. 4. TestAutoImport_SkipsWhenSiblingAgentExported passes. 5. All existing tests pass. 6. Coverage >= 87%. 7. golangci-lint clean.
nodes.creator: cli

nodes.title: Agent-native query and briefing output (FR-17)
nodes.description: Agents are writing post-processing stub code (Python/jq) to filter, project, sort, and format mtix list/search output. The fix is to expose the operations agents already perform as first-class flags and add a rendered briefing format so agents can paste output directly into their context window. See REQUIREMENTS.md FR-17 for the full specification (to be added in Phase G).

nodes.title: Multi-value filters + list/search parity (Phase A)
nodes.description: Replace single-value filter strings with comma-separated lists. Add --type flag to list. All filter values must be SQL-bound parameters.
nodes.prompt:
TDD APPROACH — write failing tests first.

TESTS:
1. TestListNodes_MultipleUnderFilter_ReturnsUnion: nodes under MUF-1, MUF-2, MUF-3. Filter Under=[MUF-1, MUF-3]. Verify only MUF-1.* and MUF-3.* returned.
2. TestListNodes_MultipleNodeTypeFilter_ReturnsUnion
3. TestListNodes_MultiplePriorityFilter_ReturnsUnion
4. TestListNodes_MultipleAssigneeFilter_ReturnsUnion
5. TestBuildFilterClauses_AllParameterized_NoConcatenation: SQL injection regression — verify args slice populated and clause strings contain only ? placeholders.
6. TestBuildFilterClauses_MultiUnder_BoundParameters
7. TestRunList_TypeFlag_FiltersResults: parity with search.
8. TestRunSearch_CommaSeparatedStatus_ParsesCorrectly
9. TestRunList_CommaSeparatedUnder_ParsesCorrectly
10. TestSplitCSVFlag_HandlesEdgeCases: empty, single, spaces, empty-between-commas.

IMPLEMENTATION:
A. internal/store/store.go NodeFilter: Under string→[]string, NodeType string→[]string, Assignee string→[]string, Priority *int→[]int. Status already []Status.
B. internal/store/sqlite/node_list.go buildFilterClauses: IN(?,?) for status/type/assignee/priority. (id=? OR id LIKE ? ESCAPE \\ OR ...) for multi-Under. ALL bound. Add comment noting LIKE prefix safety depends on FR-2.1a.
C. cmd/mtix new csv.go: splitCSV helper. Update query.go and show.go flag parsing. Add --type to list. Update runList signature.
D. Update all existing call sites passing strings to wrap as []string{value} or nil.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. All 10 new tests pass.
2. All existing tests pass.
3. golangci-lint clean.
4. Coverage on new code >= 90%.
5. mtix list --under MGIT-3,MGIT-4 --status done,cancelled --type issue works.
6. Security regression test confirms all values parameterized.
